headline: AWS Security Hub now aggregates threats across multicloud, targeting OCI customers
summary: AWS expanded Security Hub to unify security findings across multicloud environments and third-party tools from a single console. This positions AWS as a control plane for security teams running workloads on multiple clouds — including OCI. The move is a direct attempt to embed AWS tooling into mixed-cloud enterprises, increasing switching costs and AWS stickiness.
oci_implication: OCI should evaluate whether its own security observability story — native or partnered — is compelling enough to prevent AWS from becoming the de facto security layer for joint customers. This is a product gap worth assessing, particularly for enterprise accounts running workloads across both clouds.
